what_it_does: Defines an autonomous, cron-driven, parallel build loop for the Company operable composition surface, specifying lanes, verification by use plus adversarial and design-critic checks, state files, commit rules, and stopping conditions.
observations: Build runs autonomously on a */10 cron with a lock file and staleness checks.
Work is split into file-disjoint lanes (E, N, K, U, F) so they can run in parallel.
Lane U depends on K being verified, and lane F depends on C+U, though C is not listed as a lane.
Verification includes USE tests, adversarial re-check, and a separate design-critic form check.
Form is treated as half of done; a design-lint can fail the build.
Commits are limited to touched files on the operable-surface branch with no Co-Authored-By trailer.
Coordination uses .build/state.json, per-lane report files, and a loop lock.
When all criteria are terminal, it writes a WALKTHROUGH.md and notifies Tim.
standouts: File-disjoint parallel lanes with explicit dependency gating.
Mandatory adversarial re-check and separate design-critic verification.
Crash-safe overlap guard with stale lock/report reclamation.
Function-form duality enforced as a build rule rather than an afterthought.
conventions: Markdown frontmatter with name and description.
Numbered procedural steps under section headings.
Mixed Windows/Linux absolute paths for vault and repo.
Branch-specific workflow that forbids commits to main.
Single-writer report files and lock files for agent coordination.
concerns: Mixed Windows/Linux absolute paths may be environment-brittle.
20/25 minute staleness thresholds are hardcoded without rationale.
Lane F depends on C+U, but no C lane is defined in the lane list.
Autonomous agents editing code and committing without mid-loop human review carries risk.
The distinction between subjective taste and design-system violation may be hard to enforce mechanically.
notes: This is the build orchestration recipe: it tells autonomous workers what to build, how to verify it, and when to stop for Tim's review.
questions: What is the missing C lane that F depends on?
How are agent failures or merge conflicts on the shared hot files in lane K handled?
What are the exact schemas of state.json and lane report.json?
How does the design-critic operationalize the taste vs. design-system boundary?
What concrete functionality does lane F implement?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (autonomous cron-driven parallel build loop) matches the detailed procedure; because it is a specification rather than executable code, actual behavior depends on the agents and cron setup not shown here.
apparent_themes: Verification by use, not by code-reading
Fail-loud behavior with no silent fallback
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a self-driving build process that breaks a large surface into parallel, verifiable increments, with strong quality gates so only genuinely finished work reaches Tim.
novelty: The combination of file-disjoint parallel agent lanes, mandatory adversarial re-check, and a separate design-critic/form verification step embedded in the build loop is unusual for a typical CI/CD or build script.
contribution: It contributes a complete operational protocol for autonomous incremental construction: lane definitions, dependency ordering, state/lock coordination, verification rituals, commit discipline, and end-of-build handoff.
summary_for_embedding: company/docs/methodology/company-build.md specifies an autonomous cron-driven parallel build loop for the Company operable composition surface, with file-disjoint lanes, use-based verification, adversarial and design-critic checks, and a walkthrough handoff to Tim.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to define how autonomous agents should iteratively build, verify, and commit the operable composition surface without human intervention until final review.
